repo_state.notes: Workspace already had untracked option-3 artifacts and generated build output before these lane files were written.

barrier_reasons: math_backoff: full-match materialization is admitted when the match count stays at or below the threshold, then banned with exponential backoff when it exceeds the limit.
repro_scheduler_scopes: scheduler bindings are scoped through repeat/saturate blocks and run-with resolves the most recently bound scheduler name.

metrics.key_question: Do custom scheduler/backoff semantics require full-match admission boundaries?
metrics.option_implication: Custom scheduler behavior remains native-boundary evidence; DD lowering must preserve materialization, admission, and delayed firing.
metrics.performance_summary: Three scheduler/backoff runs passed in 5817000 us total.
metrics.summary: Custom scheduler/backoff lanes behaved as designed: the back-off scheduler admitted full-match materialization, then banned and later unbanned rules with exponential backoff, and scheduler scope handling stayed local to each nested schedule block.

runs.name: math_backoff
evidence: final print-size included large match counts: Add 24991, Mul 14823, MathU 2645, is-not-zero 48

runs.name: repro_scheduler_scopes
evidence: scheduler declarations inside repeat and saturate both ran successfully

runs.name: schedule_demo
evidence: left reached 10 and right reached 9 under nested saturate/repeat scheduling
the intentional fail checks still failed as expected

source_evidence.admission_rule: filter_matches chooses all matches when total_len <= threshold; otherwise it increments times_banned and bans the rule for exponentially longer intervals
source_evidence.scoping_rule: new_scope truncates the scheduler stack after repeat/seq/saturate blocks, and run-with resolves the newest scheduler binding with rfind

limitations: The experimental file harness does not initialize env_logger, so the back-off info logs were not emitted in the test output; the implementation evidence comes from the source and the observed workload results.
The `schedule_demo` run is supporting schedule-boundary evidence, not a custom back-off test.

verdict: The back-off scheduler and scheduler-scope handling both behave as intended.
